title: Combed grid pattern — marbling
sources: Josef Halfer, The Progress of the Marbling Art (1893, English translation 1894) — public domain. The grid combed pattern (also called the French pattern or double-comb) is a standard pattern in the European marbling repertoire.
notes: A basic comb-pattern tutorial: lay pigment in horizontal stripes, comb through vertically to produce the initial comb, then comb horizontally through the vertical comb to produce a grid. The grid pattern (sometimes called double-comb or French combed) is one of the most regular and geometric European marbling patterns. Covers: stripe laying (equal spacing, 3-5 colours), the first vertical comb pass, the second horizontal comb pass (tooth spacing should be the same as the vertical comb), the importance of steady, even speed. This differs from the nonpareil (which uses only one comb pass in one direction). Different tooth spacings produce fine vs bold grid.
